questions.question: Select the grammatically correct sentence.
options: I believe in the mantra that slow and steady win the race, advocating for patience and perseverance.
I believe in the mantra that slow and steady wins the race, advocating for patience and perseverance.
I believe in the mantra that slow and steady won the race, advocating for patience and perseverance.
I believe in the mantra that slow and steady winner the race, advocating for patience and perseverance.
questions.explanation: The correct answer will be: I believe in the mantra that slow and steady wins the race, advocating for patience and perseverance. According to subject verb agreement. If two or more than two nouns or adjective are joined by ‘and’ but they are talking about only person or thing then singular verb is used.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Fervent
questions.explanation: The correct answer will be: Zealous Fervent: Characterized by intense emotion or passion; enthusiastic or zealous. Zealous: Showing great enthusiasm or passion for a cause, idea, or activity; fervent. Indifferent: Showing no particular interest or concern; neither good nor bad; neutral. Apathetic: Showing or feeling no interest, enthusiasm, or concern; indifferent or unresponsive. Unconcern: Lack of interest or involvement; indifference or apathy.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. An imaginary name assumed by an author for disguise.
questions.explanation: The correct option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words "An imaginary name assumed by an author for disguise" is: Pseudonym Pseudonym: A fictitious name adopted by an author to conceal their identity. Acronym: A word formed from the initial letters or parts of a sequence of words, often used as an abbreviation. Surname: A hereditary name common to all members of a family, as distinct from a given name. Alias: An alternative name or pseudonym used by a person for character, typically to conceal their true identity.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. The scientific community eagerly awaited the revelation of the groundbreaking discovery.
questions.explanation: The correct answer will be: Concealment Revelation refers to the act of making something known or disclosed that was previously hidden, secret, or unknown. Concealment: The act of hiding something or keeping it secret. Disclosure: The act of making new or secret information known or revealing something previously hidden. Exposure: The act of making something visible or bringing it to public attention, often unintentionally. Divulgence: The act of revealing private or secret information, often willingly or deliberately.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence. Kavita was proficient in numerous languages and instruments, yet she couldn't escape the label of being a jack of all trends and master of none.
options: Skilled in many areas but not outstanding in any particular one.
Uninterested in mastering any skill or field.
Masterful in all areas but unrecognized by others.
Talented in a specific area but lacking knowledge in others.
questions.explanation: The correct answer will be: Skilled in many areas but not outstanding in any particular one. The phrase "jack of all trades, master of none" describes someone who is competent in many skills, but not an expert in any of them.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Vani is _________ a peacock in the blue satin dress.
questions.explanation: The correct answer will be: as beautiful as as ----- as is used for comparison. It is used in positive as well as negative sentences. So ----- as is used for comparison. It is used in negative sentences. Only the positive degrees of adjective and adverb can be used with ‘As ---- as’ and ‘So ---- as’.

questions.question: Select the correct spelling of the underline word. Scientists are studying seismmic waves to better understand the Earth's interior and predict future earthquakes.
questions.explanation: The correct answer will be: Seismic Seismic: connected with or caused by movements in the earth’s surface (earthquakes)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. passing the buck
questions.explanation: The most appropriate meaning of the given idiom "passing the buck" is: Blames someone else This idiom refers to shifting responsibility or blame to another person or party.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The grand jury decided to indict the suspect on charges of fraud and embezzlement.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment "to indict" in the given sentence is: To accuse Indict: to officially charge somebody with a crime. To accuse: To charge someone with wrongdoing or criminal activity, typically based on evidence or suspicion. Entrapment: The action of tricking someone into committing a crime in order to secure their prosecution. Indoctrination: The process of teaching a person or group to accept a set of beliefs or principles uncritically. Inducement: The act of persuading or influencing someone to do something, often by offering incentives or rewards.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an error. Varun had been/ waiting for his turn/ since a long time.
questions.explanation: The correct answer will be: since a long time. Here the use of since is not correct. Use for in place of since because here period of time (a long time) is given.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. Gandhi’s lawyer friends thought it would be a good idea for Andrews to stay in Champaran and help them. Andrews was willing if Gandhi agreed. Q. The cause is just and you must rely upon yourselves to win the battle. You should not seek a prop in Mr. Andrews because he happens to be an Englishman’’. R. But Gandhi was vehemently opposed. He said, ‘‘You think that in this unequal fight it would be helpful if we have an Englishman on our side. This shows the weakness of your heart. S. Early in the Champaran action, Charles Freer Andrews, the English pacifist who had become a devoted follower of the Mahatma, came to bid Gandhi farewell before going on a tour of duty to the Fiji Islands.
questions.explanation: The correct sequence of the sentences to form a coherent paragraph is (b) SPRQ. Here's how it flows logically: S: Early in the Champaran action, Charles Freer Andrews, the English pacifist who had become a devoted follower of the Mahatma, came to bid Gandhi farewell before going on a tour of duty to the Fiji Islands. P: Gandhi’s lawyer friends thought it would be a good idea for Andrews to stay in Champaran and help them. Andrews was willing if Gandhi agreed. R: But Gandhi was vehemently opposed. He said, ‘‘You think that in this unequal fight it would be helpful if we have an Englishman on our side. This shows the weakness of your heart." Q: The cause is just and you must rely upon yourselves to win the battle. You should not seek a prop in Mr. Andrews because he happens to be an Englishman’’.completing the description of the flag's design and significance.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The company's board of directors will announce the financial results at the annual meeting tomorrow.
options: The financial results will be announced by the company's board of directors at the annual meeting tomorrow.
The company's board of directors announced the financial results at the annual meeting tomorrow.
The financial results are being announced by the company's board of directors at the annual meeting tomorrow.
The financial results will have been announced by the company's board of directors at the annual meeting tomorrow.
questions.explanation: The correct option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice is: The financial results will be announced by the company's board of directors at the annual meeting tomorrow. Active voice ---- Passive voice Subject + shall/will + V1 + object ---- Object + shall/will + be + V3 + by + subject

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. In many cultures, elders are held in high veneration for their wisdom and experience.
questions.explanation: The correct answer will be: Condemnation Veneration: to honor or have great respect for a person or thing. Condemnation: The act of expressing strong disapproval or criticism; the declaration of someone's guilt or the punishment that follows. Adoration: Deep love and respect; worship or reverence towards someone or something. Esteem: High regard or respect for someone or something; admiration or honor. Deference: Respectful submission or yielding to the judgment, opinion, or will of another person, often due to their authority or superiority.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. India expected to win the World Cup.
options: The World Cup was expected to be won by India.
It was expected by India to win the World Cup
To win the World Cup has been expected by India.
India expected that the World Cup be won by it.
questions.explanation: The correct answer will be: It was expected by India to win the World Cup. Active voice – Passive voice: Subject + V2 + Object ---- Object + was/were + V3 + by + subject

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined word in the given sentence. She had an ability to impress others.
questions.explanation: The correct answer will be: persuade Impress: To make someone feel admiration and respect for you, often by doing or saying something that leaves a strong positive impact. Persuade: To convince someone to do something or to believe something through reasoning, argument, or entreaty. Outrage: An extremely strong reaction of anger, shock, or indignation, often in response to something perceived as unjust or offensive. Halt: To bring or come to a stop; to cease or discontinue movement or progress. Suppress: To forcibly put an end to something; to quash or inhibit something such as a feeling, an action, or an uprising.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. He repeated the experiment, increasing the amount until he had weighed up to a thousand pounds. B. During the spring of 1717, the iron foundries in a remote district were often visited by a thin, middle-aged man with a notebook. C. Three cauldrons were next prepared under his directions. D. He would weigh out two pounds of iron, have them heated till they were red-hot and then weigh them again.
questions.explanation: The correct order of the sentences to form a coherent paragraph is: BDAC Explanation: Sentence B sets the scene by introducing the main character and the context of the iron foundries. Sentence D follows logically after sentence B, describing the initial steps of the experiment conducted by the main character. Sentence A provides further details about the experiment, specifically mentioning the repetition and expansion of the experiment. Sentence C concludes the paragraph by describing the next step in the experiment, which is the preparation of cauldrons under the main character's direction.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word in the given sentence. The timing of the rules being notified raises a legitemate doubt if it is an attempt to divert attention away from the electoral bonds controversy.
questions.explanation: The incorrectly spelled word in the given sentence is: legitemate (should be "legitimate") legitemate (incorrectly spelled): This should be "legitimate." It means conforming to the law or to rules, valid, acceptable, or reasonable. Raises: This means to lift or move to a higher position, to cause to rise or increase, or to bring up a topic for discussion. Attention: This refers to the mental focus or concentration on someone or something, or the act of noticing or observing something. Controversy: This is a disagreement or argument involving opposing points of view, often leading to heated debate or discussion.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Coax
questions.explanation: The most appropriate synonym of "coax" from the given options is: Persuade Coaxing involves gently persuading someone to do something. Persuade: To convince someone to do something or to believe something through reasoning, argument, or entreaty. Pacify: To calm or soothe someone who is angry, agitated, or upset; to restore peace or tranquility. Punish: To impose a penalty or consequence on someone for wrongdoing; to discipline or penalize someone for an offense. Please: To give satisfaction or pleasure to someone; to make someone happy or content.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. In Bihar and Central India, in particular, every district had smelters that used local deposits of ore to produce iron which was widely used for the manufacture of implements and tools of daily use. B. The smelting was done by men while women worked on the bellows, pumping air that kept the charcoal burning. C. But iron smelting in India was extremely common till the end of the nineteenth century. D. Production of Wootz steel required a highly specialised technique of refining iron.
questions.explanation: The correct order of the sentences to form a coherent paragraph is: DCAB Explanation: Sentence D introduces the production of Wootz steel, a specialized technique in refining iron, setting the context for the subsequent sentences. Sentence C provides additional information about the prevalence of iron smelting in India until the end of the nineteenth century, expanding on the topic of iron production. Sentence A follows logically after sentence C, mentioning the specific regions in India where iron smelting was common, further developing the discussion on iron production. Sentence B concludes the paragraph by describing the process of iron smelting, specifically mentioning the roles of men and women in the production process.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate homonym to fill in the blank. The hunter dogs followed the hyena’s ________.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate homonym to fill in the blank in the given sentence is: scent scent: This refers to a distinctive smell or odor, especially one that is pleasant or alluring, often used by animals to track prey. sense: This can refer to one's ability to perceive and understand things using the five senses (sight, hearing, touch, taste, smell), or it can mean a feeling or understanding about something. cense: This is a less common word, but it can refer to the act of perfuming or burning incense as part of a religious or ceremonial ritual. cents: This is the plural form of "cent," a unit of currency equal to one hundredth of a dollar in the United States, or similar units in other currencies.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. The essence of the CAA, in reality, may not ______ the interests of minority citizens after all, it is only a fast-tracking mechanism for minorities Hindus, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis and Christians from Afghanistan, Bangladesh and Pakistan to gain citizenship. Instead of the usual 11 years (out of the preceding 14 years), the waiting period for this class of immigrants _______ only be five years, provided they had arrived before December 31, 2014. Further, there is nothing in it that removes anyone’s citizenship. The problem with the CAA is two-fold: first, in its discriminatory norm that specifies some religious communities as eligible for citizenship. All those from these six religions in these three countries are _________ to have fled persecution. Others who do not fall into this category, ________ due to their entering the country after the cut-off date, or because of their religion, will continue to be treated as illegal migrants. The second ______ is the unfortunate political propaganda that sought to link the CAA with the National Register of Citizens. This rhetoric accentuated the fears of Muslims that the CAA may lead to loss of citizenship without adequate documentary proof. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1.
questions.explanation: the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1: affect Affect: To have an influence on or produce a change in something. Effect: The result or consequence of an action or event; something brought about by a cause. Impact: The action of one object coming forcibly into contact with another; the effect or influence of one thing on another. Influence: The capacity to have an effect on the character, development, or behavior of someone or something; the power to shape decisions or actions.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. The essence of the CAA, in reality, may not ______ the interests of minority citizens after all, it is only a fast-tracking mechanism for minorities Hindus, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis and Christians from Afghanistan, Bangladesh and Pakistan to gain citizenship. Instead of the usual 11 years (out of the preceding 14 years), the waiting period for this class of immigrants _______ only be five years, provided they had arrived before December 31, 2014. Further, there is nothing in it that removes anyone’s citizenship. The problem with the CAA is two-fold: first, in its discriminatory norm that specifies some religious communities as eligible for citizenship. All those from these six religions in these three countries are _________ to have fled persecution. Others who do not fall into this category, ________ due to their entering the country after the cut-off date, or because of their religion, will continue to be treated as illegal migrants. The second ______ is the unfortunate political propaganda that sought to link the CAA with the National Register of Citizens. This rhetoric accentuated the fears of Muslims that the CAA may lead to loss of citizenship without adequate documentary proof. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 2.
questions.explanation: the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 2: will will: This option is the most appropriate because it indicates a future action with certainty, which aligns with the context of the sentence. It expresses the idea that the waiting period for immigrants will only be five years.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. The essence of the CAA, in reality, may not ______ the interests of minority citizens after all, it is only a fast-tracking mechanism for minorities Hindus, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis and Christians from Afghanistan, Bangladesh and Pakistan to gain citizenship. Instead of the usual 11 years (out of the preceding 14 years), the waiting period for this class of immigrants _______ only be five years, provided they had arrived before December 31, 2014. Further, there is nothing in it that removes anyone’s citizenship. The problem with the CAA is two-fold: first, in its discriminatory norm that specifies some religious communities as eligible for citizenship. All those from these six religions in these three countries are _________ to have fled persecution. Others who do not fall into this category, ________ due to their entering the country after the cut-off date, or because of their religion, will continue to be treated as illegal migrants. The second ______ is the unfortunate political propaganda that sought to link the CAA with the National Register of Citizens. This rhetoric accentuated the fears of Muslims that the CAA may lead to loss of citizenship without adequate documentary proof. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 3.
questions.explanation: the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 3: presumed In this context, the word "presumed" means to be believed or considered to be true without actual proof. The sentence discusses how individuals from certain religious communities in specified countries are believed to have fled persecution. This belief is based on assumptions rather than concrete evidence. Therefore, "presumed" is the most suitable option to fill in blank no. 3. 'Presume' is the word to use if you're making an informed guess based on reasonable evidence. If you're making a guess based on little or no evidence, the word to use is 'assume'. concluded: To reach a decision or form an opinion after considering all the relevant facts or evidence. speculated: To form a theory or conjecture about something without firm evidence or proof.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. The essence of the CAA, in reality, may not ______ the interests of minority citizens after all, it is only a fast-tracking mechanism for minorities Hindus, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis and Christians from Afghanistan, Bangladesh and Pakistan to gain citizenship. Instead of the usual 11 years (out of the preceding 14 years), the waiting period for this class of immigrants _______ only be five years, provided they had arrived before December 31, 2014. Further, there is nothing in it that removes anyone’s citizenship. The problem with the CAA is two-fold: first, in its discriminatory norm that specifies some religious communities as eligible for citizenship. All those from these six religions in these three countries are _________ to have fled persecution. Others who do not fall into this category, ________ due to their entering the country after the cut-off date, or because of their religion, will continue to be treated as illegal migrants. The second ______ is the unfortunate political propaganda that sought to link the CAA with the National Register of Citizens. This rhetoric accentuated the fears of Muslims that the CAA may lead to loss of citizenship without adequate documentary proof. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 4.
questions.explanation: the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 4: either "Either" is used to indicate a choice between two options. In the context of the sentence, it suggests that individuals who do not fall into the specified category may be excluded from citizenship eligibility for one of two reasons: either due to their entering the country after the cut-off date, or because of their religion. Therefore, "either" is the most suitable option to fill in blank no. 4.

questions.question: In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. The essence of the CAA, in reality, may not ______ the interests of minority citizens after all, it is only a fast-tracking mechanism for minorities Hindus, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis and Christians from Afghanistan, Bangladesh and Pakistan to gain citizenship. Instead of the usual 11 years (out of the preceding 14 years), the waiting period for this class of immigrants _______ only be five years, provided they had arrived before December 31, 2014. Further, there is nothing in it that removes anyone’s citizenship. The problem with the CAA is two-fold: first, in its discriminatory norm that specifies some religious communities as eligible for citizenship. All those from these six religions in these three countries are _________ to have fled persecution. Others who do not fall into this category, ________ due to their entering the country after the cut-off date, or because of their religion, will continue to be treated as illegal migrants. The second ______ is the unfortunate political propaganda that sought to link the CAA with the National Register of Citizens. This rhetoric accentuated the fears of Muslims that the CAA may lead to loss of citizenship without adequate documentary proof. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 5.
questions.explanation: the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 5: aspect Explanation: In this context, "aspect" refers to a particular part or feature of a situation or problem. The sentence mentions the second aspect of the problem with the CAA, which is the unfortunate political propaganda linking it with the National Register of Citizens. Therefore, "aspect" accurately represents a specific part or feature of the problem, making it the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 5.
